codeReview.summary: The worker successfully created a comprehensive 632-line Playwright test suite (13 test cases) covering the e-text agent revision browser flow. All tests pass and properly exercise the validation contract assertions: prompt submission (VAL-ETEXT-003), live progress/completion without refresh (VAL-ETEXT-004), canonical appagent attribution (VAL-CROSS-119), worker-not-author (VAL-CROSS-120), and non-duplication on renewal/retry/reload (VAL-CROSS-122). The tests follow established patterns from existing etext-authoring-history.spec.js and properly use data attributes for test selectors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the established data-attribute test selector pattern (data-etext-agent-prompt, data-etext-agent-submit, etc.) consistently with existing etext-authoring-history.spec.js patterns. No convention gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/tests/etext-agent-revision.spec.js uses data-etext-* selectors matching the existing library/etext-authoring-ui.md conventions

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker documented that agent-browser cannot handle WebAuthn ceremonies and used Playwright with virtual-authenticator instead. This is already documented in library/user-testing.md (
), but the worker's explicit note in the test comments helps reinforce this for future workers.
No new services or commands were introduced. All validation used existing services.yaml commands (go test, go vet, go build, pnpm build, playwright test).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands match existing .factory/services.yaml definitions exactly

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. Handoff reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations. The skillFeedback did not need to report any procedural issues.

summary: PASS: Comprehensive Playwright coverage added for e-text agent revision browser flow. 13 test cases cover all required validation assertions (VAL-ETEXT-003, VAL-ETEXT-004, VAL-CROSS-119, VAL-CROSS-120, VAL-CROSS-122). All 132 Playwright tests pass. No issues or risks identified. Worker followed established patterns and conventions. The feature successfully fills the browser-level proof gap that was blocking etext-desktop milestone validation.
